action.reasoning: Observation: The previous run failed with a segfault (exit code 139) and empty stdout. Analysis: An empty stdout means the script crashed before any prints were flushed to the output buffer. The segfault could be caused by importing torch_geometric, loading the dataset, or out-of-bounds indices in edge_index during the forward pass. Decision: I will rewrite train.py with `flush=True` on all prints to trace the exact line of the crash. I will also add rigorous safety checks in the Dataset `__getitem__`: casting all tensors to `.long()`, clamping `edge_index` to `[0, num_nodes - 1]`, handling empty graphs by adding a dummy node, and ensuring `edge_index` and `edge_attr` have matching sizes. I will run for 2 epochs and submit an evaluation.
